title: Attorney / Advocate
description: Represent clients in legal matters — drafting contracts, advising on rights and obligations, negotiating settlements, and appearing in court. Attorneys practise broadly; advocates specialise in litigation.

employability.demand: High — large legal services sector; demand from commerce, government, and individuals.
employability.outlook: Stable; specialised practice areas (commercial, tech, environmental law) growing.
pathways: Law firms, corporate legal, state attorney, self-employed

title: Legal Advisor / Corporate Counsel
description: Provide legal guidance to an organisation — drafting agreements, advising on compliance and risk, managing litigation, and protecting the company's legal interests in-house.

employability.demand: High — every large company and government department needs legal counsel.
employability.outlook: Growing; regulatory complexity driving demand for in-house lawyers.
pathways: Corporate legal departments, banks, insurance companies, government

title: Paralegal
description: Support lawyers with legal research, document preparation, case management, and client communication. Paralegals are essential to law firm efficiency and access-to-justice organisations.

employability.demand: Moderate — entry-level role; good pathway into the legal profession.
employability.outlook: Stable; legal tech may shift tasks but demand for human judgment remains.
pathways: Diploma in Paralegal Practice / LLB (3–4 years)
Law firms, NGOs, legal aid clinics

title: Forensic Investigator
description: Investigate financial crimes, fraud, and corruption using specialised forensic techniques — evidence gathering, interviews, financial tracing, and expert witness testimony. Bridge law and finance.

employability.demand: High — fraud and corruption investigation is a growing field in SA.
employability.outlook: Growing; public-sector accountability and anti-corruption work expanding.
pathways: Audit firms, SAPS, NPA, corporate security

title: Human Rights / Constitutional Lawyer
description: Defend constitutional rights and advance social justice through litigation, policy advocacy, and legal education. Work with NGOs, public interest law centres, and the judiciary.

employability.demand: Moderate — niche practice area; mostly NGO and public-interest sector.
employability.outlook: Stable; Constitutional Court litigation keeps demand for specialists alive.
pathways: LLM in Human Rights / Constitutional Law (1–2 years)
Section 27, SECTION27, Legal Resources Centre, public defender offices

title: Labour Law Specialist
description: Advise employers and employees on workplace rights, contracts, dismissals, and bargaining council disputes. Represent clients at the CCMA, Labour Court, and in negotiations.

employability.demand: High — high frequency of workplace disputes in SA makes labour law specialists essential.
employability.outlook: Stable; constantly evolving labour legislation keeps demand high.
pathways: Law firms, CCMA commissioners, trade unions, corporates

title: Prosecutor / State Advocate
description: Represent the state in criminal proceedings — gathering evidence, deciding on charges, and arguing cases in the Magistrates' Court and High Court on behalf of the public.

employability.demand: High — NPA has significant backlogs and needs qualified prosecutors.
employability.outlook: Stable; public-sector salary increases improving retention.

title: Magistrate / Judge
description: Preside over court proceedings, assess evidence, and deliver rulings. Magistrates handle the bulk of SA's criminal and civil cases; judges sit in higher courts on complex matters.

employability.demand: Moderate — vacancies are filled from experienced legal practitioners.
employability.outlook: Stable; judiciary is funded by government and not dependent on market cycles.
employability.employment_note: Magistrate and judge appointments require substantial prior legal practice — typically 10+ years as an attorney or advocate.
